title: L2 Regularization (Weight Decay)
concept: L2 regularization, often called weight decay, applies a smooth pull toward smaller weights, shrinking oversized connections while usually keeping them active.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Why do we penalize very large weights in the first place?

options.text: Because giant weights often amplify noise and lead to overfitting.
Because large weights prevent backpropagation from existing.
Because every useful feature must have weight zero.

exercises.explain: Yes. Regularization starts from the idea that huge weights often make a network brittle and overly specific to the training set.

cards.title: A spring pulling back to center
cards.body: Picture a joystick with a soft spring inside it. You can push it in any direction, but the farther it drifts, the more the spring pulls it back toward center. Weight decay gives neural-network weights that same centering pull.

cards.title: What L2 regularization does
cards.body: L2 regularization, often called weight decay, adds an extra cost for large weights. Because the penalty grows smoothly and hits larger weights harder, the optimizer keeps nudging oversized connections downward instead of letting them dominate.

cards.title: Magnets on every slider
cards.body: Imagine a wall of sliders, each with a small magnet pulling it toward the middle. Sliders that move far away feel the strongest pull. L2 works similarly: every weight can move, but drifting too far becomes increasingly uncomfortable.

cards.title: Big weight, bigger pull
cards.body: Suppose one hidden-layer connection has grown much larger than the others. Under weight decay, that oversized connection feels a stronger push back toward a safer size, while smaller useful weights only get a light tug and can keep helping.

cards.title: Weight decay is not the same as deleting features
cards.body: A common myth is that L2 removes connections completely. Usually it does not. Its style is gentle shrinkage: many weights stay alive, but none are allowed to become wildly dominant.

cards.title: How weight decay treats different weights
cards.body: L2 gives every weight a pull toward smaller values, but the biggest weights feel that pull most strongly.

left.text:
Feels a strong pull back toward center.
Shrinks faster if it grows too dominant.

right.text:
Feels only a light pull.
Can stay if it keeps helping the prediction.

exercises.prompt: What is the main effect of L2 regularization in a deep network?

options.text: It smoothly shrinks weights, especially the largest ones.
It forces every weight to jump straight to zero.
It removes the need for a validation set.
It replaces backpropagation with random search.

exercises.explain: Exactly. L2 regularization applies smooth pressure toward smaller values, with the biggest weights receiving the strongest correction.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Weight decay usually keeps many connections non-zero while shrinking them.
exercises.explain: True. L2 is known for gentle shrinkage, not hard pruning.

exercises.sentence: Weight decay is another common name for ___ regularization.
exercises.explain: Correct. Weight decay is the everyday name for L2 regularization in many deep-learning settings.

exercises.prompt: Which result sounds most like L2 regularization at work?

options.text: Most weights become smaller, but many still stay active and non-zero.
Every weak connection disappears exactly at once.
The model keeps a few giant weights and makes them even larger.

exercises.explain: Right. L2 usually produces a dense but more controlled network, not a fully pruned one.

exercises.prompt: A sound-classification network uses many useful frequency clues, but a few weights keep growing much larger than the rest and validation accuracy starts wobbling. Which regularization choice fits best?

options.text: Use L2 weight decay so the oversized weights shrink while the many helpful small clues stay in play.
Use a rule that forces every connection to zero as soon as it changes.
Remove the loss function and keep only the penalty.
Increase the biggest weights because they are already winning.

exercises.explain: Perfect. When many clues matter but some weights are getting too dominant, L2 is a natural fit because it reins in those large connections without deleting the whole support system.
